market_summary: S&P 500 hit a fresh all-time record close of 7,137.90 on April 22 (+1.04% from April 21 close of 7,064.01), driven by Trump indefinitely extending the Iran ceasefire and strong Q1 earnings beats (3 of 4 S&P 500 companies exceeding expectations). Nasdaq gained +1.64% to 24,657.57 — tech reclaiming leadership. However, Iran simultaneously attacked 3 commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22 hours after the ceasefire extension, exposing the fragile nature of the geopolitical situation. The 'Great Rotation' out of tech appears to be reversing — CNBC headlined 'The much-hyped great rotation out of tech for 2026 may be over already' on April 22. MILESTONE this week: ASML exit successfully executed and ~$150 of proceeds redeployed into CSPX, resolving the multi-report structural underweight.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Ceasefire indefinitely extended by Trump on April 22, but Iran attacked 3 commercial ships the same day. Hormuz remains effectively closed with $1M+ tolls per vessel; US naval blockade continues. GLD 4-week risk-on clock has NOT started — Iran's continued ship attacks confirm the conflict is not resolved. Goldman Sachs projects Brent at ~$80/barrel year-end (~$20 above pre-war baseline). 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — peak driven by Iran war energy costs, gasoline +18.9% YoY. Core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12, expected to peak near 4% before declining toward 3% if Hormuz resolves. 3. FED: Rates held at 3.50-3.75%; April 28-29 FOMC described as 'an easy call' to hold. JPMorgan forecasts no cuts in 2026. U03A stage-down trigger contingent on dovish FOMC signal — unlikely to fire given JPM hawkish outlook. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA confirmed $1T+ Blackwell + Vera Rubin backlog through 2027 (doubled in one year). AMZN Q1 earnings on April 29 — AWS growth expected to accelerate to 28-30%. NVDA up 18% in 10 days. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Tech reclaiming leadership from Energy. 'Great Rotation' into value/energy appears to be reversing. IT, Financials, Materials showing pro-cyclical earnings support. 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues with no new escalation this week.
portfolio_health: Grade: B (80/100). NAV $4,288.17. Week return -0.08% vs S&P 500 +1.04% = -1.12pp underperformance — entirely attributable to transaction costs from ASML exit + CSPX add commissions (~$8-12 friction); underlying position performance was positive (+~$30 from price appreciation). MILESTONE: ASML exit executed and ~$150 of proceeds redeployed into CSPX. CSPX now at 38.70% of holdings NAV (35.68% of total NAV) — at/above the 36% holdings target for the first time. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.32%. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only) — excellent overall structure. Key upcoming catalysts: FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29, MA earnings April 30, May 3 cash deployment hard deadline. GLD on WATCH (3.6% buffer above $420 trim trigger, improved from 2.3% at April 21 close). NVDA STRONGEST position — up 18% in 10 days.

positions.rationale: CSPX has crossed the 36% holdings target (now 38.70% of holdings NAV, 35.68% of total NAV) following the ASML exit and redeployment. S&P 500 at fresh records and tech rotation returning to leadership reinforce the core anchor thesis. Remaining cash (~$234 post-MA conditional add) to be deployed here by May 3 hard deadline. IE-domiciled accumulating structure remains optimal for WHT purposes. No urgency to add beyond the May 3 deployment.

Q1 2026 earnings on April 29 at 5:30 PM ET. AWS growth expected to accelerate to 28-30% (from 24% in Q4). BofA EBIT estimate $21.4B vs Street $20.7B. Trainium custom silicon at >$10B run rate growing triple-digit YoY. 42 Buys / 3 Holds, average target $286.23 (+12.1% from $255.36). Conditional ADD $50-75 post-earnings if AWS >25% AND EBIT >$20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised. Pre-earnings RSI elevated — hold current position.

Iran attacked 3 commercial ships on April 22 hours after ceasefire extension — Hormuz remains functionally closed. The 4-week risk-on invalidation clock has NOT started. GLD at $435.26, 3.6% above the $420 trim trigger (improved from 2.3% at April 21 close). Goldman $5,400 year-end target; central bank demand ~850T/year; all macro thesis pillars intact. Daily close alert at $420 maintained — trim 25% of position (~$109) if breached and redeploy into CSPX.

Q1 2026 earnings on April 30. Consensus EPS $4.40 (+18% YoY). Market trading below the $520-$600 premium range pending Q1 cross-border volume confirmation. Conditional ADD $100 on April 30 if: cross-border volume >14% AND VAS >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. Average analyst target $652.66 (+27.9% upside from $510.16). Hold ahead of the decisive April 30 catalyst.

Highest-conviction position — NVDA up 18% in 10 days. $1T+ Blackwell + Vera Rubin backlog through 2027 (doubled in one year). Vera Rubin in production for H2 2026 delivering 10x performance/watt over Grace Blackwell. Next earnings May 27, 2026 (corrected from prior May 20 reference); consensus $1.75 EPS. No add at current elevated levels — original add target was $180-185, now passed. Let position compound through May 27 earnings catalyst.

Capital preservation layer with YTM 3.68% (above 3.0% trim trigger). Fed expected to hold at April 28-29 FOMC with no dovish signal (JPM forecasts no 2026 cuts). Stage-down of -$100 into CSPX is CONTINGENT on dovish FOMC signal — if hawkish/hold-unchanged, no U03A action. Iran ship attacks on April 22 reinforce near-term capital preservation rationale. If FOMC dovish: immediately trim $100 and redeploy into CSPX.

rebalancing: ASML exit successfully executed and approximately $150 of proceeds redeployed into CSPX (1.8025 -> 1.9989 shares). CSPX is now at 38.70% of holdings NAV — the multi-report structural underweight has been fully closed. Remaining cash $334.40 (7.80% of NAV) to be deployed as follows: $100 conditional on MA April 30 earnings beat criteria, remaining ~$234 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline (unconditional). FOMC April 28-29: if dovish, additionally trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). NVDA and AMZN both carry STRONGER/STRONGEST thesis flags — no reductions warranted. Portfolio structure is clean with the ASML drag eliminated.
cash_strategy: Cash $334.40 (7.80% of NAV) is transitional post-ASML-exit dry powder. Deployment plan: (1) Reserve $100 for conditional MA add on April 30 earnings beat; (2) Deploy remaining ~$234 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline unconditionally (retain $50 minimum buffer); (3) If FOMC dovish on April 28-29, additionally trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). Cash must not exceed 5% of NAV after May 3. If MA add condition not met: deploy full ~$284 into CSPX by May 3.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate — AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator partnerships through 2029-2031), VMware networking dominance, AI chip revenue expected to double in Q1 2026. Negligible dividend = 0% effective WHT. The 'Great Rotation' reversal back to tech makes timing increasingly attractive. Gate: NAV must exceed $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,288 is $712 below the threshold.

action_items: 1. IMMEDIATE WATCH — April 28-29 FOMC: If dovish signal (rate cut window opened or cutting bias expressed), trim U03A -$100 and deploy into CSPX immediately. If hawkish or hold-unchanged: no U03A action.

2. CRITICAL — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings (5:30 PM ET): ADD $50-75 post-earnings if AWS growth >25% AND EBIT >$20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. No exit unless severe guide-down.

3. CRITICAL — April 30 MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate partial reduce only on material miss with guide-down.

4. HARD DEADLINE — May 3: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. If MA add executed: deploy ~$184; if not: deploy ~$284. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions under any circumstances.
5. MONITOR — GLD $420 daily close alert: GLD at $435.26, only 3.6% above trim trigger. If daily close below $420: trim 25% of GLD (~$109) and redeploy into CSPX immediately.

6. WATCH — Iran developments: Iran attacked 3 ships on April 22 despite ceasefire extension. Any formal peace deal + durable Hormuz reopening starts the 4-week GLD invalidation clock. Clock has NOT started.

7. MONITOR — NVDA May 27 earnings: Q1 FY2027 consensus $1.75 EPS / $78.8B revenue. Watch HBM supply constraint update for Vera Rubin production ramp. NOTE: Earnings date is May 27 (corrected from prior May 20 reference in thesis).
